patches.hypothesis: The ArrayStoreException occurs because the array type created is not compatible with the objects being stored in it. The issue arises from using the class of the first element in 'singletons' which may not match the type of objects returned by 'sample()'. We should use the class of the objects returned by 'sample()' instead.
patches.changes:
- final T[]out = (T[]) java.lang.reflect.Array.newInstance(singletons.get(0).getClass(), sampleSize);
+ final T[]out = (T[]) java.lang.reflect.Array.newInstance(sample().getClass(), sampleSize);
patches.diff:
- final T[]out = (T[]) java.lang.reflect.Array.newInstance(singletons.get(0).getClass(), sampleSize);
+ final T[]out = (T[]) java.lang.reflect.Array.newInstance(sample().getClass(), sampleSize);

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by creating an array of a specific subclass of Object instead of the raw Object type, which leads to an ArrayStoreException when trying to store different instances in the array.
patches.changes:
- final T[]out = (T[]) java.lang.reflect.Array.newInstance(singletons.get(0).getClass(), sampleSize);
+ final T[] out = (T[]) new Object[sampleSize];
patches.diff:
- final T[]out = (T[]) java.lang.reflect.Array.newInstance(singletons.get(0).getClass(), sampleSize);
+ final T[] out = (T[]) new Object[sampleSize];
